after,holding,bound,after,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must be false at all time points with distance <=$duration$ from the trigger (i.e., trigger, trigger+1, ..., and trigger+$duration$). Moreover, $post_condition$ must hold at the point that is at distance $duration$+1 from the trigger, that is, trigger+$duration$+1, except if the end of the interval occurs earlier.

after,holding,bound,always,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must hold at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.

after,holding,bound,before,satisfaction.pctl: P>=1[(((! FLin_$scope_mode$) U (FLin_$scope_mode$ & (X (G ($regular_condition$ => (P$bound$[(($post_condition$ | LAST) R (! $stop_condition$))])))))) | (G (! FLin_$scope_mode$)))]
after,holding,bound,before,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must hold at least once strictly before the state where the stop condition holds. If the stop condition never occurs in the interval, $post_condition$ does not need to hold. If the stop condition holds at the trigger, the requirement is not satisfied.

after,holding,bound,eventually,satisfact.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must hold at some time point between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.

after,holding,bound,finally,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, if trigger holds, then $post_condition$ holds at the end of the interval.

after,holding,bound,for,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must hold at the trigger and remain true for $duration$ time points past the trigger (i.e., trigger, trigger+1, ..., and trigger+$duration$), or to the end of the interval if the interval ends sooner than trigger+$duration$.

after,holding,bound,immediately,satisfac.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, if trigger holds then $post_condition$ also holds at the same time point.

after,holding,bound,never,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must be false at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.

after,holding,bound,next,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:

after,holding,bound,null,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:

after,holding,bound,until,satisfaction.pctl: P>=1[(((! FLin_$scope_mode$) U (FLin_$scope_mode$ & (X (G ($regular_condition$ => (P$bound$[(($stop_condition$ R ($post_condition$ | $stop_condition$)) | (LAST R $post_condition$))])))))) | (G (! FLin_$scope_mode$)))]
after,holding,bound,until,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) starting strictly after the first $scope_mode$ interval and spanning to the end of the execution.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must remain true until (but not necessarily including) the point where the stop condition holds, or to the end of the interval. If the stop condition never occurs, $post_condition$ must hold until the end of the scope, or forever. If the stop condition holds at the trigger, the requirement is satisfied.

in,regular,bound,within,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in every interval where $scope_mode$ holds.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval if $regular_condition$ is true and any point in the interval where $regular_condition$ becomes true (from false).
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must hold at some point with distance <= $duration$ from the trigger (i.e., at trigger, trigger+1, ..., or trigger+$duration$). If the interval ends sooner than trigger+$duration$, then $post_condition$ need not hold.

in,regular,null,within,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in every interval where $scope_mode$ holds.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval if $regular_condition$ is true and any point in the interval where $regular_condition$ becomes true (from false).
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, $post_condition$ must hold at some point with distance <= $duration$ from the trigger (i.e., at trigger, trigger+1, ..., or trigger+$duration$). If the interval ends sooner than trigger+$duration$, then $post_condition$ need not hold.

notin,holding,bound,after,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in every interval where $scope_mode$ does NOT hold.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must be false at all time points with distance <=$duration$ from the trigger (i.e., trigger, trigger+1, ..., and trigger+$duration$). Moreover, $post_condition$ must hold at the point that is at distance $duration$+1 from the trigger, that is, trigger+$duration$+1, except if the end of the interval occurs earlier.

notin,holding,null,until,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in every interval where $scope_mode$ does NOT hold.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, $post_condition$ must remain true until (but not necessarily including) the point where the stop condition holds, or to the end of the interval. If the stop condition never occurs, $post_condition$ must hold until the end of the scope, or forever. If the stop condition holds at the trigger, the requirement is satisfied.

notin,holding,null,within,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in every interval where $scope_mode$ does NOT hold.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, $post_condition$ must hold at some point with distance <= $duration$ from the trigger (i.e., at trigger, trigger+1, ..., or trigger+$duration$). If the interval ends sooner than trigger+$duration$, then $post_condition$ need not hold.

notin,null,bound,after,satisfaction.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in every interval where $scope_mode$ does NOT hold.
TRIGGER: first point in the interval.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must be false at all time points with distance <=$duration$ from the trigger (i.e., trigger, trigger+1, ..., and trigger+$duration$). Moreover, $post_condition$ must hold at the point that is at distance $duration$+1 from the trigger, that is, trigger+$duration$+1, except if the end of the interval occurs earlier.

TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must be false at least once strictly before the state where the stop condition holds. If the stop condition never holds in the interval, $post_condition$ need not be false at least once.

onlyAfter,holding,bound,within,satisfact.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) from the start of the execution to (and including) the first interval where $scope_mode$ holds. In the interval defined by the entire execution, if $scope_mode$ never holds.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability $probability_bound_expression$, $post_condition$ must be false at all time points with distance <=$duration$ from the trigger (i.e., at trigger, trigger+1, ..., and trigger+$duration$), or until the end of the interval.

onlyAfter,holding,null,after,satisfactio.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) from the start of the execution to (and including) the first interval where $scope_mode$ holds. In the interval defined by the entire execution, if $scope_mode$ never holds.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, $post_condition$ must either hold at some point with distance <=$duration$ from the trigger (i.e., at trigger, trigger+1, ..., or trigger+$duration$), or $post_condition$ must remain false for $duration$+1 time points starting at the trigger (i.e., at trigger, trigger+1, ..., and trigger+$duration$+1), or to the end of the interval if it ends sooner than trigger+$duration$+1.

onlyAfter,holding,null,always,satisfacti.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) from the start of the execution to (and including) the first interval where $scope_mode$ holds. In the interval defined by the entire execution, if $scope_mode$ never holds.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, $post_condition$ must be false at some time point between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.

onlyAfter,holding,null,before,satisfacti.pctl: P>=1[(FLin_$scope_mode$ | (FLin_$scope_mode$ R ($regular_condition$ => (P>=1[(! ((! (((! $stop_condition$) & (! FLin_$scope_mode$)) U ($post_condition$ & (! $stop_condition$)))) | (! (((! ((! $post_condition$) & FLin_$scope_mode$)) & (! FLin_$scope_mode$)) U $post_condition$))))]))))]
onlyAfter,holding,null,before,satisfacti.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) from the start of the execution to (and including) the first interval where $scope_mode$ holds. In the interval defined by the entire execution, if $scope_mode$ never holds.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, $post_condition$ must be false until, but not necessarily including, the state where the stop condition holds. If the stop condition never occurs in the interval, $post_condition$ cannot occur.

onlyAfter,holding,null,eventually,satisf.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) from the start of the execution to (and including) the first interval where $scope_mode$ holds. In the interval defined by the entire execution, if $scope_mode$ never holds.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, $post_condition$ must be false at all time points between (and including) the trigger and the end of the interval.

onlyAfter,holding,null,finally,satisfact.probabilistic_description:
SCOPE: in the interval (if defined) from the start of the execution to (and including) the first interval where $scope_mode$ holds. In the interval defined by the entire execution, if $scope_mode$ never holds.
TRIGGER: every point in the interval where $regular_condition$ is true.
REQUIRED BEHAVIOR: for every trigger, with probability >=1, trigger and $post_condition$ cannot hold at the last time point of the interval.
